lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Watch. Engy will solve this little maze.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Done! That was easy.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: But a model with no memory is stateless — afterwards, the notebook empties.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: ...Have I seen this before?

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Why does it feel so hard again?

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: With no memory, every task starts from zero. You can't keep what you can't remember.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: So we give Engy a memory module — a place to keep and reuse what it learns.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Now the note survives. Stored experience can be reused.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Wait — I've done something like this before...

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Faster this time! Memory helps.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: It does. But look closely at the box itself...

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Who designed these slots — what to keep, where to file it, what to forget?

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: For years, we drew the memory design by hand — what to store, how to fetch it, when to update.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Hand-crafted for one task, it fits beautifully.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: This world is... different. Nothing lines up.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: A fixed design can't adapt to new, changing tasks.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Diverse, shifting tasks each want a different memory. One hand-drawn shape can't serve them all.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: So... what if the design didn't have to be drawn by hand?

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: This old design only scored 0.3. Why?

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: What if we change how it retrieves and updates?

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Try it on a real task: 0.3 → 0.4.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Keep the result. Sample again. This open-ended loop never stops improving.

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: A memory design is just... code.

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: A list? A database? A graph? Code can build any of them.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Install the new design straight into Engy's memory.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: This shape suits this task!

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: And the space of possible memories is endless.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Every design ever tried is kept here — with its score.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: 0.2... 0.4... 0.7 — the archive remembers what worked.

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: Start from a design that already did well.

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: ...then make it better.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: File the result back. Good designs seed even better ones.

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: This is open-ended search — it compounds. It never has to stop.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Four very different worlds: ALFWorld · TextWorld · Baba Is AI · MiniHack.

lines.speaker: metaagent
lines.label: Meta Agent
lines.text: Each world, its own memory design.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: I'm actually learning from experience now!

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Across every benchmark, the learned designs beat the hand-crafted ones.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: And they cost less to run.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Better memory — discovered, not hand-drawn.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: My memory isn't fixed anymore. It can grow.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: A step toward AI that learns to be a continual learner — designing its own memory.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Powerful. Which is exactly why it must be built and deployed safely.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: And this is the road ENGRAM walks: dated states, arriving in order.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Read only up to today. Write it down. Answer — without peeking ahead.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Remember the right things, at the right time. That's the whole challenge.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Today's agents don't do one step. They walk long roads — many steps, far from where they began.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Find, open, read, fix, check, report... that's a lot to hold onto.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Each step becomes a note in memory.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: The start feels so far away now...

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Will I still remember how this began?

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: On a long road, memory is what keeps you coherent. So — how do we test it?

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Old memory tests look like conversations.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: But an agent's real memory is a trajectory of states, actions, observations, tool outputs.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: JSON, tables, code — machine-generated, not free-form chatter.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: And it's causal: each action changes the world, which shapes what comes next.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Dense and objective — no small talk to wade through.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: So a fair test must use trajectories. That's what AMA-Bench does.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: AMA-Bench has two halves.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Real-world: six domains — Web, open-world QA, Text2SQL, software engineering, gaming, embodied AI.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Real trajectories, with questions written and checked by experts.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Synthetic: rule-generated tasks in programmatic environments.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: And synthetic tasks scale to any horizon length.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Realistic and scalable — that's the design.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: I ask four kinds of question.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: Temporal — what happened, and in what order?

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: State dependency — given what you did before, what's true now?

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: Memory updating — the value changed. What is it now?

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: Summarization — distill the whole journey, faithfully.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Four ways for my memory to be tested... or to fail.

lines.speaker: examiner
lines.label: Examiner
lines.text: Why is the gate open now?

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Similarity search grabs the note that looks alike — and misses the one that actually caused it.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: Lossy compression had already thrown away the exact detail.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Um... it was always open?" (wrong) — Examiner: "Incorrect.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Even the best models reach only ~72%. Many memory systems do worse than just keeping everything in context.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: The model isn't the weak link. The memory design is — it loses the causal, objective facts.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: What if memory kept the causes, not just look-alike notes?

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: AMA-Agent builds a causality graph: action → state change → observation.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: And I can use a tool to fetch the exact node — by following the cause, not the resemblance.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: The gate is open because I unlocked it three steps back." — Examiner: "Correct.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: 57.22% — eleven points over the best baseline.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Keep the causes. Retrieve with tools. That's the difference.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: The lesson: it's not the size of the model. It's the design of the memory.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Keep the causes, retrieve well — design is everything.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.label: (caption)
lines.text: And there's a challenge built on exactly this...

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: ENGRAM: read the dated states in order — never peek at tomorrow.

lines.speaker: engy
lines.label: Engy
lines.text: Is this fact current? stale? superseded? uncertain? — and here's my evidence.

lines.speaker: sensei
lines.label: Sensei
lines.text: Measure memory honestly. Then build one worth measuring. That's ENGRAM.
